revision.comment: /* Using example-formatting templates in general */ Added an introductory description of what is displayed with the basic {{xt}} template.
revision.text: {{lowercase title}} {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:xt]], [[Template:bxt]], [[Template:mxt]], [[Template:xtd]], [[Template:bxtd]], [[Template:!xt]], [[Template:!xts]], [[Template:!bxt]], and [[Template:!mxt]]}} {{distinguish|Category:Text color templates}} {{High-use}} This and related templates produce an inline typeface change suitable to render e'''X'''ample '''T'''ext on [[Help:Contents|Wikipedia's help pages]]. ==Using example-formatting templates in general== {{mbox | type = content | text = '''Not for use in article space''': This family of templates cannot be used in [[Wikipedia:Mainspace|mainspace]] (the article [[Wikipedia:Namespace|namespace]]). }} {{lua|Module:TEMPLATENAME|noprotcat=yes}} The [[:Category:Example-formatting templates|category of example-formatting templates]] is used frequently in non-[[Wikipedia:Mainspace|mainspace]] general [[Help:Contents|help pages]] such as [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Wikipedia:Manual of Style]] (MOS) when ''inline'' typeface changes are needed. {{code|{{((}}xt{{))}}}} changes the typeface to serif and the text color to green. For cases where the serif typeface is not desirable (e.g. in blocks of computer code), use {{tlx|bxt}}, which substitutes boldfacing, or {{tlx|mxt}}, which substitutes a monospaced font. For style examples that break to their own line (e.g. paragraphs), use {{tlx|xt2}}. For examples of {{!xt|bad/wrong style, in red}}, use {{tlx|!xt}}, {{tlx|!bxt}}, {{tlx|!mxt}}, or {{tlx|!xt2}}, respectively to the previous paragraph (the addition of the [[Negation#Programming language and ordinary language|!]] indicates the opposite effect). Use {{tlx|!xts}} for bad/wrong style {{!xts|with strikethrough formatting}}. The {{tlx|xtd}} template exists for {{xtg|deprecated}} examples. Its alias {{tlx|xtg}} (for "grey") can be used to indicate uncertain, unavailable, disabled, lorem, etc., examples without implying [[deprecation]]. The bold, sans-serif equivalent is {{tlx|bxtd}} (and {{tlx|bxtg}} alias). The mono-spaced equivalent is {{tlx|mxtd}}. The {{tlx|xtn}} template is for {{xtn|neutral}} examples, and has no color change, but is otherwise identical to {{tlx|xt}}. It can be used with {{tnull|xt}} and {{tnull|!xt}} to indicate usage that is neither advised nor advised against or deprecated. The bold, sans-serif equivalent is {{tlx|bxtn}}. ==Parameters== * {{para||{{var|text here}}}} or {{para|1|{{var|text here}}}} – The text to which to apply the markup. As with all templates, when the text has any equals characters ("="), explicitly prefix the text with {{para|1}} or the template will break. * {{para|title|{{var|tooltip text here}}}} – Takes text, ''which cannot be marked up in any way'', and displays it as a pop-up "tooltip" (in most browsers) when the cursor hovers over the span ==Accessibility== The accompanying change in typeface to a serif or monospaced type style (as in {{xt|example text}}) is to make it fully accessible for those with [[color blindness]]. Keep [[WP:Accessibility|accessibility]] more broadly in mind, and never construct examples such that a blind person, who may not be able to see the coloration, boldfacing, or monospace font change, cannot understand the examples. Especially indicate, with terms like "not", "don't", "wrong:", etc., that a negative example is a negative one, or with "deprecated", "avoid", etc., that deprecated material is deprecated. ==Examples of {{[[Template:xt|xt]]}} syntax and result== ;What you write: :...for example, {{xt|1=''T'' = 293.15 K}}, but not {{!xt|1=''m''=5.4kg}}. ;What you get: :...for example, {{xt|1=''T'' = 293.15 K}}, but not {{!xt|1=''m''=5.4kg}}. {| class=wikitable !Template class !Output typeface |- |{{tlx|xt}} |{{xt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|!xt}} |{{!xt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|xtn}} |{{xtn|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|xtd}} |{{xtd|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|mxt}} |{{mxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|!mxt}} |{{!mxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|mxtn}} |{{mxtn|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|mxtd}} |{{mxtd|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|bxt}} |{{bxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|!bxt}} |{{!bxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|bxtn}} |{{bxtn|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|bxtd}} |{{bxtd|inline typeface change}} |} == User CSS for a monospaced coding font == {{collapse top|left=y|title=Change system-default monospace font:}} {{Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font}} {{collapse bottom}} ==See also== {{Xt/see also}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Example-formatting templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }}

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing misc last stuff
revision.text: {{lowercase title}} {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:xt]], [[Template:bxt]], [[Template:mxt]], [[Template:xtd]], [[Template:bxtd]], [[Template:!xt]], [[Template:!xts]], [[Template:!bxt]], and [[Template:!mxt]]}} {{distinguish|Category:Text color templates}} {{High-use}} This and related templates produce an inline typeface change suitable to render e'''X'''ample '''T'''ext on [[Help:Contents|Wikipedia's help pages]]. ==Using example-formatting templates in general== {{mbox | type = content | text = '''Not for use in article space''': This family of templates cannot be used in [[Wikipedia:Mainspace|mainspace]] (the article [[Wikipedia:Namespace|namespace]]). }} {{lua|Module:TEMPLATENAME|noprotcat=yes}} The [[:Category:Example-formatting templates|category of example-formatting templates]] is used frequently in non-[[Wikipedia:Mainspace|mainspace]] general [[Help:Contents|help pages]] such as [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Wikipedia:Manual of Style]] (MOS) when ''inline'' typeface changes are needed. {{code|{{((}}xt{{))}}}} changes the typeface to serif and the text color to green. For cases where the serif typeface is not desirable (e.g. in blocks of computer code), use {{tlx|bxt}}, which substitutes boldfacing, or {{tlx|mxt}}, which substitutes a monospaced font. For style examples that break to their own line (e.g. paragraphs), use {{tlx|xt2}}. For examples of {{!xt|bad/wrong style, in red}}, use {{tlx|!xt}}, {{tlx|!bxt}}, {{tlx|!mxt}}, or {{tlx|!xt2}}, respectively to the previous paragraph (the addition of the [[Negation#Programming language and ordinary language|!]] indicates the opposite effect). Use {{tlx|!xts}} for bad/wrong style {{!xts|with strikethrough formatting}}. The {{tlx|xtd}} template exists for {{xtg|deprecated}} examples. Its alias {{tlx|xtg}} (for "grey") can be used to indicate uncertain, unavailable, disabled, lorem, etc., examples without implying [[deprecation]]. The bold, sans-serif equivalent is {{tlx|bxtd}} (and {{tlx|bxtg}} alias). The mono-spaced equivalent is {{tlx|mxtd}}. The {{tlx|xtn}} template is for {{xtn|neutral}} examples, and has no color change, but is otherwise identical to {{tlx|xt}}. It can be used with {{tnull|xt}} and {{tnull|!xt}} to indicate usage that is neither advised nor advised against or deprecated. The bold, sans-serif equivalent is {{tlx|bxtn}}. ==Parameters== * {{para||{{var|text here}}}} or {{para|1|{{var|text here}}}} – The text to which to apply the markup. As with all templates, when the text has any equals characters ("="), explicitly prefix the text with {{para|1}} or the template will break. * {{para|title|{{var|tooltip text here}}}} – Takes text, ''which cannot be marked up in any way'', and displays it as a pop-up "tooltip" (in most browsers) when the cursor hovers over the span ==Accessibility== The accompanying change in typeface to a serif or monospaced type style (as in {{xt|example text}}) is to make it fully accessible for those with [[color blindness]]. Keep [[WP:Accessibility|accessibility]] more broadly in mind, and never construct examples such that a blind person, who may not be able to see the coloration, boldfacing, or monospace font change, cannot understand the examples. Especially indicate, with terms like "not", "don't", "wrong:", etc., that a negative example is a negative one, or with "deprecated", "avoid", etc., that deprecated material is deprecated. ==Examples of {{[[Template:xt|xt]]}} syntax and result== ;What you write: :...for example, {{xt|1=''T'' = 293.15 K}}, but not {{!xt|1=''m''=5.4kg}}. ;What you get: :...for example, {{xt|1=''T'' = 293.15 K}}, but not {{!xt|1=''m''=5.4kg}}. {| class=wikitable !Template class !Output typeface |- |{{tlx|xt}} |{{xt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|!xt}} |{{!xt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|xtn}} |{{xtn|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|xtd}} |{{xtd|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|mxt}} |{{mxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|!mxt}} |{{!mxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|mxtn}} |{{mxtn|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|mxtd}} |{{mxtd|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|bxt}} |{{bxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|!bxt}} |{{!bxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|bxtn}} |{{bxtn|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|bxtd}} |{{bxtd|inline typeface change}} |} == User CSS for a monospaced coding font == {{collapse top|left=y|title=Change system-default monospace font:}} {{Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font}} {{collapse bottom}} ==See also== {{Xt/see also}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Example-formatting templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:bump-01-18]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text: {{lowercase title}} {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:xt]], [[Template:bxt]], [[Template:mxt]], [[Template:xtd]], [[Template:bxtd]], [[Template:!xt]], [[Template:!xts]], [[Template:!bxt]], and [[Template:!mxt]]}} {{distinguish|Category:Text color templates}} {{High-use}} This and related templates produce an inline typeface change suitable to render e'''X'''ample '''T'''ext on [[Help:Contents|Wikipedia's help pages]]. ==Using example-formatting templates in general== {{mbox | type = content | text = '''Not for use in article space''': This family of templates cannot be used in [[Wikipedia:Mainspace|mainspace]] (the article [[Wikipedia:Namespace|namespace]]). }} {{lua|Module:TEMPLATENAME|noprotcat=yes}} The [[:Category:Example-formatting templates|category of example-formatting templates]] is used frequently in non-[[Wikipedia:Mainspace|mainspace]] general [[Help:Contents|help pages]] such as [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Wikipedia:Manual of Style]] (MOS) when ''inline'' typeface changes are needed. {{code|{{((}}xt{{))}}}} changes the typeface to serif and the text color to green. For cases where the serif typeface is not desirable (e.g. in blocks of computer code), use {{tlx|bxt}}, which substitutes boldfacing, or {{tlx|mxt}}, which substitutes a monospaced font. For style examples that break to their own line (e.g. paragraphs), use {{tlx|xt2}}. For examples of {{!xt|bad/wrong style, in red}}, use {{tlx|!xt}}, {{tlx|!bxt}}, {{tlx|!mxt}}, or {{tlx|!xt2}}, respectively to the previous paragraph (the addition of the [[Negation#Programming language and ordinary language|!]] indicates the opposite effect). Use {{tlx|!xts}} for bad/wrong style {{!xts|with strikethrough formatting}}. The {{tlx|xtd}} template exists for {{xtg|deprecated}} examples. Its alias {{tlx|xtg}} (for "grey") can be used to indicate uncertain, unavailable, disabled, lorem, etc., examples without implying [[deprecation]]. The bold, sans-serif equivalent is {{tlx|bxtd}} (and {{tlx|bxtg}} alias). The mono-spaced equivalent is {{tlx|mxtd}}. The {{tlx|xtn}} template is for {{xtn|neutral}} examples, and has no color change, but is otherwise identical to {{tlx|xt}}. It can be used with {{tnull|xt}} and {{tnull|!xt}} to indicate usage that is neither advised nor advised against or deprecated. The bold, sans-serif equivalent is {{tlx|bxtn}}. ==Parameters== * {{para||{{var|text here}}}} or {{para|1|{{var|text here}}}} – The text to which to apply the markup. As with all templates, when the text has any equals characters ("="), explicitly prefix the text with {{para|1}} or the template will break. * {{para|title|{{var|tooltip text here}}}} – Takes text, ''which cannot be marked up in any way'', and displays it as a pop-up "tooltip" (in most browsers) when the cursor hovers over the span ==Accessibility== The accompanying change in typeface to a serif or monospaced type style (as in {{xt|example text}}) is to make it fully accessible for those with [[color blindness]]. Keep [[WP:Accessibility|accessibility]] more broadly in mind, and never construct examples such that a blind person, who may not be able to see the coloration, boldfacing, or monospace font change, cannot understand the examples. Especially indicate, with terms like "not", "don't", "wrong:", etc., that a negative example is a negative one, or with "deprecated", "avoid", etc., that deprecated material is deprecated. ==Examples of {{[[Template:xt|xt]]}} syntax and result== ;What you write: :...for example, {{xt|1=''T'' = 293.15 K}}, but not {{!xt|1=''m''=5.4kg}}. ;What you get: :...for example, {{xt|1=''T'' = 293.15 K}}, but not {{!xt|1=''m''=5.4kg}}. {| class=wikitable !Template class !Output typeface |- |{{tlx|xt}} |{{xt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|!xt}} |{{!xt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|xtn}} |{{xtn|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|xtd}} |{{xtd|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|mxt}} |{{mxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|!mxt}} |{{!mxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|mxtn}} |{{mxtn|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|mxtd}} |{{mxtd|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|bxt}} |{{bxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|!bxt}} |{{!bxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|bxtn}} |{{bxtn|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|bxtd}} |{{bxtd|inline typeface change}} |} == User CSS for a monospaced coding font == {{collapse top|left=y|title=Change system-default monospace font:}} {{Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font}} {{collapse bottom}} ==See also== {{Xt/see also}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Example-formatting templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] }}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:{{subst:FULLPAGENAME}}]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text: {{lowercase title}} {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:xt]], [[Template:bxt]], [[Template:mxt]], [[Template:xtd]], [[Template:bxtd]], [[Template:!xt]], [[Template:!xts]], [[Template:!bxt]], and [[Template:!mxt]]}} {{distinguish|Category:Text color templates}} {{High-use}} This and related templates produce an inline typeface change suitable to render e'''X'''ample '''T'''ext on [[Help:Contents|Wikipedia's help pages]]. ==Using example-formatting templates in general== {{mbox | type = content | text = '''Not for use in article space''': This family of templates cannot be used in [[Wikipedia:Mainspace|mainspace]] (the article [[Wikipedia:Namespace|namespace]]). }} {{lua|Module:TEMPLATENAME|noprotcat=yes}} The [[:Category:Example-formatting templates|category of example-formatting templates]] is used frequently in non-[[Wikipedia:Mainspace|mainspace]] general [[Help:Contents|help pages]] such as [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Wikipedia:Manual of Style]] (MOS) when ''inline'' typeface changes are needed. {{code|{{((}}xt{{))}}}} changes the typeface to serif and the text color to green. For cases where the serif typeface is not desirable (e.g. in blocks of computer code), use {{tlx|bxt}}, which substitutes boldfacing, or {{tlx|mxt}}, which substitutes a monospaced font. For style examples that break to their own line (e.g. paragraphs), use {{tlx|xt2}}. For examples of {{!xt|bad/wrong style, in red}}, use {{tlx|!xt}}, {{tlx|!bxt}}, {{tlx|!mxt}}, or {{tlx|!xt2}}, respectively to the previous paragraph (the addition of the [[Negation#Programming language and ordinary language|!]] indicates the opposite effect). Use {{tlx|!xts}} for bad/wrong style {{!xts|with strikethrough formatting}}. The {{tlx|xtd}} template exists for {{xtg|deprecated}} examples. Its alias {{tlx|xtg}} (for "grey") can be used to indicate uncertain, unavailable, disabled, lorem, etc., examples without implying [[deprecation]]. The bold, sans-serif equivalent is {{tlx|bxtd}} (and {{tlx|bxtg}} alias). The mono-spaced equivalent is {{tlx|mxtd}}. The {{tlx|xtn}} template is for {{xtn|neutral}} examples, and has no color change, but is otherwise identical to {{tlx|xt}}. It can be used with {{tnull|xt}} and {{tnull|!xt}} to indicate usage that is neither advised nor advised against or deprecated. The bold, sans-serif equivalent is {{tlx|bxtn}}. ==Parameters== * {{para||{{var|text here}}}} or {{para|1|{{var|text here}}}} – The text to which to apply the markup. As with all templates, when the text has any equals characters ("="), explicitly prefix the text with {{para|1}} or the template will break. * {{para|title|{{var|tooltip text here}}}} – Takes text, ''which cannot be marked up in any way'', and displays it as a pop-up "tooltip" (in most browsers) when the cursor hovers over the span ==Accessibility== The accompanying change in typeface to a serif or monospaced type style (as in {{xt|example text}}) is to make it fully accessible for those with [[color blindness]]. Keep [[WP:Accessibility|accessibility]] more broadly in mind, and never construct examples such that a blind person, who may not be able to see the coloration, boldfacing, or monospace font change, cannot understand the examples. Especially indicate, with terms like "not", "don't", "wrong:", etc., that a negative example is a negative one, or with "deprecated", "avoid", etc., that deprecated material is deprecated. ==Examples of {{[[Template:xt|xt]]}} syntax and result== ;What you write: :...for example, {{xt|1=''T'' = 293.15 K}}, but not {{!xt|1=''m''=5.4kg}}. ;What you get: :...for example, {{xt|1=''T'' = 293.15 K}}, but not {{!xt|1=''m''=5.4kg}}. {| class=wikitable !Template class !Output typeface |- |{{tlx|xt}} |{{xt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|!xt}} |{{!xt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|xtn}} |{{xtn|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|xtd}} |{{xtd|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|mxt}} |{{mxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|!mxt}} |{{!mxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|mxtn}} |{{mxtn|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|mxtd}} |{{mxtd|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|bxt}} |{{bxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|!bxt}} |{{!bxt|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|bxtn}} |{{bxtn|inline typeface change}} |- |{{tlx|bxtd}} |{{bxtd|inline typeface change}} |} == User CSS for a monospaced coding font == {{collapse top|left=y|title=Change system-default monospace font:}} {{Mxt/User CSS for a monospaced coding font}} {{collapse bottom}} ==See also== {{Xt/see also}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Example-formatting templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:{{subst:FULLPAGENAME}}]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] }}

revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Xt/doc]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]<!--redirecting existing page-->

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] to [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace {{subst:NAMESPACE}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Xt/doc]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace Template]][[Category:qq]] <!--redirecting existing page-->

revision.comment: tagging these as blank templates

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qq" to "11"
revision.text: #[[en:Template:Xt/doc]] [[Category:Pages with blank templates]]<noinclude>[[Category:Blank templates]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]]</noinclude> [[Category:Pages with blank templates]]<noinclude>[[Category:Blank templates]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]]</noinclude>[[Category:Pages with blank templates]]<noinclude>[[Category:Blank templates]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] <noinclude>[[Category:Pages tagged as templates in userspace Template]]</noinclude>[[Category:11]] </noinclude> <!--ing existing page-->

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Category:Pages with blank templates" to "Category:Erroneous transclusions of {{subst:PAGENAME}}"

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Refreshing s 1-26
revision.text: #[[en:Template:Xt/doc]] <noinclude></noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude><noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude> </noinclude> <!--ing existing page-->

revision.comment: Bot: tag as missing wikidata (no wikidata found via interwikis)
revision.text: #[[en:Template:Xt/doc]] <noinclude></noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude><noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude> </noinclude> <!--ing existing page--> <noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: #[[en:Template:Xt/doc]] <noinclude></noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude><noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude> </noinclude> <!--ing existing page-->

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: #[[en:Template:Xt/doc]] <noinclude></noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude><noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude> </noinclude> <!--ing existing page--> [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23694499169|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: #[[en:Template:Xt/doc]] <noinclude></noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude><noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude> </noinclude> <!--ing existing page--> <noinclude> [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "</noinclude>↵<noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: #[[en:Template:Xt/doc]] <noinclude></noinclude><noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude> </noinclude> <!--ing existing page--> <noinclude> [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/25015587690|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
#[[en:Template:Xt/doc]] <noinclude></noinclude><noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude> </noinclude>
